breaking.text: Treasury Sec. Bessent confirms 15% global tariff takes effect this week, replacing prior 10% rate under Section 122

summary: U.S. stocks rallied on Wednesday as easing oil prices and strong economic data offset Middle East war concerns — the S&P 500 gained 0.78% to 6,869.50, the Dow added 238 points (+0.49%), and the Nasdaq surged 1.29%. Tech and semiconductors led the advance with Micron and AMD each jumping over 5%, while crypto stocks exploded higher (Coinbase +14.6%, MicroStrategy +10.4%) after President Trump signaled support for digital asset legislation and Bitcoin broke above $73,000. After the close, Broadcom delivered a blowout Q1 with $19.3B revenue (+29% YoY) and AI revenue soaring 106% to $8.4B, guiding next quarter to $22B vs $20.6B consensus — shares rose 5% after hours. Treasury Secretary Bessent confirmed that 15% global tariffs will be imposed this week under Section 122, though he predicted rates would return to pre-Supreme Court levels within five months.

highlights.text: Key economic data due March 5: Initial Jobless Claims (8:30 AM), Q4 revised Unit Labor Costs & Nonfarm Productivity, and Challenger Job Cuts report
StubHub posts $1.9B net loss in first post-IPO earnings on $1.4B stock-based compensation charge, but guides FY2026 to $9.9-10.1B GMS and $400-420M adjusted EBITDA
Treasury Secretary Bessent says 15% global tariffs under Section 122 take effect this week, but predicts rates return to pre-Supreme Court ruling levels within 5 months
Veeva Systems beats Q4 estimates with $836M revenue (+13% YoY) and $2.06 EPS vs $1.93 consensus, maintaining 40%+ operating margins — shares up 4% after hours
Broadcom CEO Hock Tan outlines path to $100B in AI chip revenue, citing progress with Anthropic, Meta, and OpenAI as key custom accelerator customers
